canonical_definition: The degree to which an attention layer contributes meaningful change to the hidden representation during inference, inferred from how much the representation shifts across the layer.
papers: SqueezeAttention: 2D Management of KV-Cache in LLM Inference via Layer-wise Optimal Budget
Streamlining Redundant Layers to Compress Large Language Models
